chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Bail Appl.No.643 of 2026 Date Order with signature of the Judge Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro. Mr. Justice Khalid Hussain Shahani. Ameen Shah..............Vs........................ The State 23.04.2026. Mr. Javed Ahmed Shar Advocate for applicant Ms. Rubina Qadir, DPG. a/w SIP Gulzar Hussain P.S. Shah Latif Town. O R D E R = MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Applicant was arrested by a police team of P.S. Shah Latif Town Karachi, from double road opposite Fast University, Shah Latif Town, District Malir Karachi on 30.10.2025 on suspicion and from him allegedly 1010 grams of Charas was recovered, hence was booked in FIR bearing Cr. No.1620/2025 U/s 9 3(c), Sindh, CNS Act, 2025 of P.S. Shah Latif Town, Karachi. 2. Learned counsel in defence has pleaded for bail on the ground that applicant is the first offender; is in jail for more than six months and FIR and mashirnama of arrest and recovery do not show of USB pertaining to video recording of the incident in compliance of section 17 Sindh CNS Act. He further submits that only in Challan, mention of USB transpires which shows that evidence in shape of USB has been contrived. 3. On the other hand, learned Add.P.G. has opposed the bail. I.O. is present and submits that USB was handed over to him in sealed condition afterwards and he has not even seen it. 4. We have considered submissions of the parties and are of the view that case against applicant requires further inquiry as prima facie evidence of USB was produced to I.O., after necessary documents had already been prepared viz. FIR and mashirnama of arrest and recovery, which prima facie do not contain any mention of USB.

We have considered submissions of the parties and are of the view that case against applicant requires further inquiry as prima facie evidence of USB was produced to I.O., after necessary documents had already been prepared viz. FIR and mashirnama of arrest and recovery, which prima facie do not contain any mention of USB. Even otherwise, opinion of the I.O. for referring the applicant to trial is not based on USB as he has not seen it. More so, applicant is the first offender and is not required for 2 further investigation as Challan has been submitted. Accordingly, this application is allowed and applicant is granted bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the trail court. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. The Cr. Bail Application is disposed of. JUDGE JUDGE A.K
